title: Jet Aviation to Provide Sustainable Aviation Fuel During World Economic Forum (WEF) 2026
content: Jet Aviation announced that it will provide sustainable aviation fuel during the World Economic Forum 2026 in Zurich, marking the fifth year it has supported SAF availability as part of ongoing efforts to enable more sustainable choices for customers. Jet Aviation was the first to make SAF available in Switzerland for the World Economic Forum in 2020, with onâ€‘site supply during WEF 2023, 2024, and 2025, and via Book & Claim in 2022. SAF is widely recognized as having the potential to lower lifecycle emissions by up to 80 percent compared to conventional jet fuel. Joao Martins said they are delighted to offer SAF onâ€‘site again in Davos and highlighted expanding access in Switzerland, including a permanent supply at the global headquarters in Basel, thanking partners for their collaboration. Jet Aviation supports access through onâ€‘site availability at 13 facilities worldwide and a global Book & Claim program for locations without physical supply. President Jeremie Caillet emphasized an effortless customer experience and broader sustainability actions such as building to sustainable standards, electric charging, electric ground equipment, recycling, waste management and reduced singleâ€‘use plastics, plus bioâ€‘based resin innovation.
author: Analysts Account
